proposals.title: Multi-Parent Recombination
proposals.rationale: Enable evolutionary leaps that combine proven traits from distinct active lineages while preserving a verifiable ancestry DAG rather than limiting progress to single-parent mutation.
acceptance: A fixed ordered parent set, crossover plan, and seed always produce the same child digest.
Validation rejects recombination receipts containing unknown, duplicate, or inactive parent digests.
Accepted receipts record every parent digest, the crossover plan, seed, and resulting child digest.
Lineage artifacts and the observatory render recombination as a multi-parent DAG without rewriting prior receipts.

proposals.title: Stagnation Detection and Recovery
proposals.rationale: Detect plateaus within comparable fitness epochs and trigger bounded exploratory recovery before repeated weakest-first attempts exhaust budget without advancing the frontier.
acceptance: Stagnation is computed deterministically from a configured number of comparable attempts within one fitness epoch and selection policy.
Cross-epoch observations are never combined when calculating plateau status.
A plateau triggers only the configured bounded recovery strategy and never relaxes validation or admission thresholds.
Recovery decisions record the triggering evidence, policy digest, consumed budget, and outcome in the evolution ledger.

proposals.title: Adaptive Mutation Operators
proposals.rationale: Turn accumulated evolution outcomes into operator-level learning so the harness increasingly selects mutation strategies that produce accepted gains for each niche and fitness epoch.
acceptance: Every generated proposal records a stable mutation-operator identifier and operator version.
Accepted and rejected eligible proposals update deterministic operator statistics without altering historical receipts.
Operator credit is isolated by fitness epoch and quality-diversity niche.
Given identical ledger state and seed, operator selection and proposal parameters are reproducible.

proposals.title: Lineage Diversity Preservation
proposals.rationale: Complement phenotype-based quality-diversity with genealogical distance so one successful family cannot silently dominate the active frontier and eliminate independent evolutionary options.
acceptance: Genealogical relatedness is derived deterministically from immutable parent links.
The lineage-diversity policy distinguishes close relatives from phenotypically similar organisms with independent ancestry.
Frontier replacement preserves the configured minimum number of independent families whenever qualifying alternatives exist.
The observatory displays family representation and the ancestry evidence behind lineage-diversity decisions.

proposals.title: Evolvability-Aware Selection
proposals.rationale: Reward lineages that consistently generate stronger descendants, not merely organisms with high immediate fitness, to sustain improvement as the frontier approaches local optima.
acceptance: Evolvability is calculated from a fixed, policy-bounded probe budget using versioned metrics and recorded seeds.
Probe offspring cannot enter the frontier unless they independently pass the normal atomic admission transaction.
Selection uses evolvability only as the configured secondary criterion and never bypasses the fitness margin.
Replaying the same lineage state, metric version, and seeds reproduces identical evolvability scores and selection decisions.

proposals.title: Bounded consensus runoff
proposals.rationale: Repeated no-consensus artifacts indicate the exact-eight council needs a controlled convergence phase without weakening independence or autonomy limits.
acceptance: A failed first ballot triggers at most one policy-bounded runoff.
All eight strategies rank the same digest-addressed first-round candidate set against the unchanged repository snapshot.
Aggregation is deterministic under reordered inputs.
Ties, missing quorum, or exhausted budgets fail closed with a recorded reason and start no implementation.

proposals.title: Policy-aware intelligence provider routing
proposals.rationale: The harness exposes local brainstem, Copilot CLI, and seeded paths, but deterministic readiness checks and policy-governed handoff would prevent ad hoc or unsafe fallback.
acceptance: Each provider exposes a bounded machine-readable readiness and capability preflight.
Automatic mode selects the first ready provider from a policy-ordered allowlist.
Fallback occurs only for classified availability failures, never after invalid proposals or referee rejection.
Provider selection, handoffs, budget consumption, and sanitized diagnostics are recorded without secrets.

proposals.title: Crash-resumable orchestration runs
proposals.rationale: Atomic warehouse writes protect data, but process interruption can still duplicate expensive council or improver calls unless orchestration progress is checkpointed.
acceptance: Checkpoints persist run ID, snapshot revision, policy digest, completed call digests, and consumed budgets atomically.
Resuming never repeats a completed provider call or accepted mutation.
An interrupted and resumed run produces the same deterministic decision payload as an uninterrupted run.
Corrupt checkpoints or revision and policy mismatches fail closed.

proposals.title: Single-writer orchestration leases
proposals.rationale: Local commands and GitHub workflows can overlap; atomic mutations alone do not prevent duplicate model spend or competing runs against the same revision.
acceptance: A revision-bound lease is acquired before any provider call or mutation.
A competing run for the same operation and revision exits before consuming provider budget.
Expired leases require deterministic stale-owner recovery and are audit-recorded.
GitHub workflows share a concurrency group, while optimistic revision checks reject stale publication.

proposals.title: Scoped human approval gates
proposals.rationale: Publishing currently requires editing the global autonomy policy; one-time action-bound approvals would permit narrow reach-up without broadly expanding autonomous authority.
acceptance: A denied external side effect can emit a pending descriptor without executing it.
Only an operator command can issue an approval bound to the action digest, repository revision, expiry, and policy digest.
Expired, altered, or replayed approvals are rejected before side effects.
Execution receipts reference the approval digest without exposing credentials or signing material.

proposals.title: Least-privilege GitHub Actions boundary
proposals.rationale: Public issue ingestion and autonomous harness workflows cross untrusted input into repository mutation. Explicit token scopes, immutable action references, and injection-safe input handling limit compromise impact.
acceptance: Every workflow defaults to read-only permissions and grants write scopes only to the specific mutation job.
Fork and pull-request jobs receive neither write-capable tokens nor repository secrets.
Every external action reference is pinned to a full commit SHA, with CI rejecting mutable tags or branches.
Issue fields containing shell metacharacters or newlines are processed as data without executing commands.

proposals.title: Operator-controlled autonomy policy integrity
proposals.rationale: Bounded autonomy cannot be authoritative if an autonomous run can modify the policy that grants its own permissions. A protected ceiling must remain outside the candidate workspace's control.
acceptance: The effective policy is the intersection of the workspace policy and an operator-protected policy ceiling.
Editing the workspace policy to enable publishing while the protected ceiling denies it fails before any network side effect.
Policy parse, version, integrity, or protected-revision failures deny all side effects.
Receipts record digests for both the requested policy and the protected ceiling used for authorization.

proposals.title: Signing-key rotation and revocation
proposals.rationale: Signed lineage operations need an explicit signer trust lifecycle; otherwise unknown, expired, or compromised keys can remain authoritative indefinitely.
acceptance: Verification rejects signatures from unknown keys or keys lacking the required operation scope.
Signer records define validity and revocation boundaries using ledger revisions rather than untrusted wall-clock claims.
A rotation fixture verifies historical operations under the prior key and new operations only under the replacement key.
Revoking a key causes operations at or after the revocation boundary to fail closed in both ledger verification and promotion.

proposals.title: Immutable submission provenance verification
proposals.rationale: Public gist URLs and issue fields are untrusted, mutable references. Ingestion should bind each submission to authenticated GitHub identities, an immutable gist revision, and verified bytes.
acceptance: Ingestion resolves gists only through GitHub's API and fetches the exact selected revision without following arbitrary user-supplied URLs.
The trusted event actor's numeric ID must match the gist owner or an explicitly delegated submitter.
Each ingest receipt records issue identity, actor ID, gist ID, gist revision, and content digest.
Private, deleted, redirected, oversized, ownership-mismatched, or revision-raced submissions are rejected without changing warehouse state.

proposals.title: Pinned and sandboxed hologram runtime
proposals.rationale: Content-addressed Moment tokens are rendered by active code at a mutable external URL. Pinning renderer provenance and constraining iframe capabilities prevents historical art drift and limits hostile renderer behavior.
acceptance: Every generated card records an immutable renderer revision and asset digest in the build manifest.
Generated iframe URLs use an immutable renderer deployment; CI rejects mutable root or latest-version URLs.
Every renderer iframe uses a sandbox that permits scripts but excludes same-origin access, top navigation, popups, forms, and downloads.
A hostile renderer fixture cannot read the parent, navigate the top-level page, open a popup, or receive a referrer.

proposals.title: Seeded Daily Evolution Challenges
proposals.rationale: Give every player the same reproducible parent, constraints, and scoring target each day, creating short competitive sessions without altering the canonical frontier.
acceptance: A UTC date and pinned frontier revision deterministically produce one challenge ID, parent, seed, fitness version, and modifier set.
A run completes only when its child passes validation and every declared challenge objective.
Challenge receipts record the challenge ID, controls, score, and resulting child digest.
Completed challenges remain replayable after the active frontier changes.
The UI clearly distinguishes personal best, challenge target, and invalid attempts.

proposals.title: Trait Locks and Mutation Budget
proposals.rationale: Let players deliberately preserve valued traits and distribute finite mutation effort across motion, articulation, and radiance instead of choosing only among opaque drafts.
acceptance: Players can lock supported genome traits and allocate a fixed mutation budget among motion, articulation, and radiance.
Generated drafts preserve every locked value exactly.
The same parent, seed, locks, and budget produce identical drafts.
Impossible or over-budget configurations are rejected before an evolution transaction begins.
Accepted evolution receipts include the selected locks and budget allocation.

proposals.title: Ranked Triple-Jump Seasons
proposals.rationale: Turn isolated tournaments into sustained competition through rule-pinned seasons, deterministic standings, and immutable champion archives.
acceptance: Each season manifest pins its schedule, frontier revision, fitness epoch, tournament rules, and scoring formula.
Only signed, replay-verifiable results matching the season manifest enter standings.
Standings use documented deterministic tie-breakers and rebuild identically from accepted receipts.
Closed-season standings and champion records cannot be modified by later frontier changes.
The arena displays current rank, score gaps, season deadline, and archived seasons.

proposals.title: Receipt-Backed Evolution Campaign
proposals.rationale: Create a structured mastery path whose objectives teach evolution mechanics and whose progress can be reconstructed from genuine play rather than mutable browser flags.
acceptance: Campaign chapters define ordered, testable objectives using validated evolution or tournament receipts.
A chapter unlocks only after all prerequisite objectives are satisfied.
Importing the same receipt set reconstructs identical campaign progress on another device.
Campaign reset does not mutate warehouse organisms, lineage history, or canonical receipts.
Unlocks add modes, modifiers, or presentation options without changing canonical fitness calculations.

proposals.title: Three-Creature Draft Tournaments
proposals.rationale: Add strategic competition around the existing population by making players assemble budget-limited squads and choose lineups for contrasting performance rounds.
acceptance: A legal squad contains exactly three distinct organisms from a pinned frontier and stays within the declared strength budget.
A match has three published scoring rounds, and each drafted organism must be used exactly once.
Match outcomes and tie-breakers are deterministic from the two squad manifests and rules digest.
Players can compete against deterministic built-in squads or an imported opponent squad code.
Every result exposes a replay showing lineup choices, round scores, and the final winner.

proposals.title: Viewport-aware hologram loading
proposals.rationale: Pages presenting many live hologram iframes need bounded rendering, memory, and network work to remain responsive on mobile devices.
acceptance: Hologram iframe URLs are not assigned until their placeholders approach the viewport.
Mobile viewports attach no more than four hologram iframes concurrently, without evicting focused content.
Placeholders preserve layout dimensions and expose accessible loading, failure, and retry states.
Automated browser tests verify bounded iframe counts and no horizontal overflow at 375 CSS pixels.

proposals.title: Accessible arena controls and fallback
proposals.rationale: The 3D arena's pointer-driven experience needs equivalent keyboard, touch, screen-reader, and WebGL-independent paths to its ranked organisms and Evolve action.
acceptance: Every organism can be selected and evolved using only a keyboard outside the 3D canvas.
Selection announces the organism name, rank, and strength through an ARIA live region.
Interactive touch targets are at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels and do not require precision gestures.
When WebGL is unavailable, a functional ranked HTML view and Evolve action remain available.

proposals.title: Motion and playback controls
proposals.rationale: Persistent controls for animation and rendering intensity reduce vestibular discomfort, battery use, and mobile data consumption without removing core functionality.
acceptance: The default mode follows prefers-reduced-motion, while an explicit user override persists across pages.
Users can choose full, reduced, or paused motion through keyboard- and screen-reader-operable controls.
Reduced or paused mode suppresses autoplaying holograms and nonessential 3D or CSS motion while preserving all actions and information.
Save-Data mode begins with static previews and requires explicit activation of live holograms.

proposals.title: Shareable and restorable URL state
proposals.rationale: Stable deep links make selected organisms, filters, and comparison context shareable and prevent navigation or refresh from discarding user progress.
acceptance: Selected organisms and applicable filter or sort state are encoded using stable content identifiers.
Reloading or opening a copied URL restores the same view and moves focus to the restored context.
Browser Back and Forward restore state without duplicate history entries or full-page reloads.
Unknown or retired identifiers produce an actionable notice instead of a blank or broken view.

proposals.title: Frontend health and diagnostics
proposals.rationale: A static client application needs visible failures and privacy-preserving diagnostics so data, rendering, and performance regressions do not appear as unexplained empty screens.
acceptance: Data loading and rendering emit structured local events containing page, phase, duration, outcome, and build revision.
Network, schema, and render failures show distinct messages with retry and copy-diagnostics actions.
Diagnostics report dataset revisions and counts while excluding URL query values, hashes, and Moment payloads.
Automated tests simulate failed and malformed responses and verify that no page silently renders an empty success state.

proposals.title: Uncertainty-Bounded Fitness
proposals.rationale: Versioned fitness remains a point estimate that can reward candidates tuned to exact sampling boundaries. Evaluate a deterministic perturbation ensemble and select on a conservative bound so brittle metric exploits cannot clear the leapfrog margin.
acceptance: Every evaluation records the version and digest of a finite deterministic scenario set plus nominal, minimum, and conservative aggregate scores.
A candidate is rejected when its required leapfrog margin disappears under the conservative aggregate, even if its nominal score passes.
Repeated evaluation of the same candidate and scenario digest produces byte-identical results.
Fixtures demonstrate that a boundary-tuned candidate fails while a consistently stronger candidate passes.

proposals.title: Fitness Drift Monitoring
proposals.rationale: Historical optimization can silently saturate terms, collapse descriptors, or shift score meaning without violating individual receipts. Epoch-level drift telemetry makes these Goodhart signals visible and can halt unhealthy autonomous selection.
acceptance: The audit emits deterministic per-epoch score quantiles, gate-hit rates, clipping rates, component shares, and descriptor occupancy.
Thresholds are versioned and severe drift prevents another autonomous jump with an explicit diagnostic.
Synthetic saturation, single-component dominance, and niche-collapse histories trigger their corresponding diagnostics.
A healthy fixture remains below every configured drift threshold, and the observatory displays each metric with its policy threshold.

proposals.title: Pareto Fitness Selection
proposals.rationale: A single blended quality score permits an extreme strength to compensate for a catastrophic weakness. Bounded Pareto selection within quality-diversity niches preserves explainable tradeoffs while rejecting objectively dominated candidates.
acceptance: Fitness evaluation emits a versioned objective vector whose components and normalization rules are included in receipts.
A candidate dominated on every objective is rejected regardless of its blended score.
A nondominated tradeoff candidate can coexist within a niche up to a documented cap, after which deterministic pruning applies.
Replay reconstructs identical dominance relations, pruning decisions, and frontier ordering.

proposals.title: Explainable Fitness Scorecards
proposals.rationale: Scalar scores obscure which metric, gate, or penalty decided an evolution. Canonical scorecards expose contribution arithmetic and local sensitivity, making suspicious wins auditable without weakening deterministic replay.
acceptance: Every evaluated candidate receives a canonical scorecard containing raw metrics, normalized contributions, gates, penalties, and final score.
Scorecard arithmetic reconstructs the recorded final score within the evaluator's documented tolerance.
Receipts bind the scorecard digest, and ledger verification fails after any scorecard field is altered.
The observatory identifies the decisive contribution and displays parent-versus-child component deltas without requiring source-code inspection.

proposals.title: Behavioral Novelty Archive
proposals.rationale: Fixed quality-diversity niches can be gamed at bin boundaries and forget behaviors that leave the active frontier. A continuous, immutable historical novelty archive rewards genuinely distinct trajectories while retaining the existing quality floor.
acceptance: Each candidate receives a deterministic behavioral signature and nearest-neighbor distance against immutable historical signatures.
Small perturbations across a niche boundary cannot create a discontinuous novelty increase above the configured tolerance.
Metadata-only changes and descriptor-near trajectory clones are not admitted as novel.
Novel candidates must still satisfy the active quality floor, and replay reproduces archive insertion and distance calculations exactly.

proposals.title: Required CI quality gates
proposals.rationale: State-mutating workflows should execute only after the exact revision passes deterministic tests, integrity audits, and artifact reproducibility checks.
acceptance: CI runs unit and integration tests, ledger/frontier/lineage verification, fitness parity checks, and two byte-identical clean artifact builds.
Harness and ingest mutation jobs depend on a successful gate for the same commit SHA.
A workflow contract test proves pre-gate jobs have no write-scoped repository permissions and corrupted fixtures prevent mutation jobs from starting.

proposals.title: Scheduled-run concurrency control
proposals.rationale: Atomic writes do not prevent overlapping scheduled, manual, and ingest runs from wasting provider calls or racing branch updates.
acceptance: All warehouse-mutating workflows share a repository-wide concurrency group with in-progress runs preserved rather than cancelled.
Two runs targeting one frontier revision produce exactly one execution claim before any provider or council call; the loser exits as an explicit no-op.
A simulated interrupted claim becomes reclaimable after its lease expires without duplicating observations, lineage receipts, or mutations.

proposals.title: Missed-run detection and bounded catch-up
proposals.rationale: Hosted cron is best-effort, so skipped evolution cycles need durable detection and policy-bounded recovery instead of remaining invisible.
acceptance: Configured UTC schedule slots receive deterministic IDs and cannot execute more than once.
Slots overdue beyond a configured grace period are recorded as missed and surfaced by the workflow.
Catch-up processes oldest missed slots first while respecting autonomy limits for rounds, provider calls, and runtime, leaving excess backlog pending.

proposals.title: Operational telemetry and health alerting
proposals.rationale: Receipts prove decisions but do not provide run-level health, latency, failure classification, budget consumption, or actionable degradation signals.
acceptance: Every automated run emits a schema-versioned record containing run ID, commit, trigger, state revisions, phase durations, consumed budgets, outcome, and error class.
Telemetry serialization tests prove credentials, prompts, and provider responses cannot enter operational records.
The health check fails with a clear annotation for stale schedules, consecutive failures, or threshold breaches and deduplicates any policy-authorized issue alert.

proposals.title: Verified backup and restore drills
proposals.rationale: Git history is the primary record, but operators still need an independently verified recovery path for branch loss, corruption, or accidental deletion.
acceptance: Backup generation produces a manifest containing the source commit, schema versions, and SHA-256 digest of every canonical state file.
Restore stages into an empty destination, verifies every digest and repository invariant, and publishes restored state only after all checks pass.
Corrupt, incomplete, path-traversing, or incompatible backups are rejected without changing the destination.
A scheduled recovery drill restores the latest backup into temporary storage and runs the full integrity suite.

proposals.title: Guided First Evolution
proposals.rationale: Turn the existing gallery, arena, lab, and observatory into one coherent first-run journey without expanding the core evolution engine.
acceptance: A clean browser session can start a guided evolution from the primary landing-page CTA without CLI use or authentication.
The tour introduces the target, fitness margin, candidate comparison, selection, and receipt in context.
A deterministic demo mode never publishes or mutates repository data.
The tour supports keyboard navigation, restart, skip, and mobile layouts.

proposals.title: Federation Compatibility Manifest
proposals.rationale: Make the advertised peer federation dependable by exposing compatibility, provenance, and freshness instead of treating every remote catalog as equally usable.
acceptance: Each peer manifest declares a stable peer ID, catalog revision, supported schemas, fitness epochs, and signing keys.
CI rejects duplicate peer IDs, insecure URLs, malformed manifests, and unsupported catalog schemas.
The Holodex labels every remote card with origin, compatibility, signature status, and last verified revision.
An unavailable or invalid peer cannot remove local cards or prevent other valid peers from loading.

proposals.title: Harness Project Scaffolding
proposals.rationale: Productize the generic harness so users can apply Double Jump beyond Moments without reverse-engineering its scorer, population, and policy contracts.
acceptance: One documented command creates a runnable project with a seed population, scorer, autonomy policy, and usage instructions.
The generated project completes a deterministic dry-run and emits a verifiable evolution receipt.
Generation refuses to overwrite existing files unless an explicit force option is supplied.
Invalid scorer output produces contract-specific diagnostics and a nonzero exit status.

proposals.title: Moment Import and Preview Workflow
proposals.rationale: Give creators a safe path from portable RAPP Moment JSON to preview and submission while reusing the repository's strict validation and deduplication foundations.
acceptance: Users can paste or upload Moment JSON and preview it locally before any network action.
Validation failures identify exact field paths and match the ingest tool's conformance rules.
Valid imports display content digest, fitness epoch, score, and duplicate status.
Submission requires an explicit action and generates an issue payload whose digest matches tools/ingest.py.

proposals.title: Cross-Surface Lineage Deep Links
proposals.rationale: Connect the product's exploration surfaces around one canonical artifact identity so creatures and their evolution stories are directly shareable.
acceptance: Every artifact view supports one documented digest-based URL format.
Links between gallery, arena, Holodex, lab, and observatory preserve the selected artifact digest.
Active and retired artifacts resolve to their immutable lineage records.
Malformed or unknown digests show an explicit error instead of silently selecting another artifact.
